signals.reasoning: BTC is already in a liquidation-flush state, so I would not chase a fresh short after the initial dump.
ETH is also already flushing and does not offer a clean new edge in either direction right here.
SOL has already moved into flush territory, so I would rather wait for a bounce than force a new short here.
VVV remains the clearest short because price is still down hard while open interest is building again, which suggests the downside move is still active.
COMP remains the only meaningful long candidate because funding is still deeply negative while price is holding up, though liquidity keeps confidence capped.
BLUR is too mixed and too low-liquidity to justify a serious fresh trade.
TON has shifted from catch-down fade to watch-only because it is still green and now showing strong open-interest support, making a fresh short less attractive here.
BIO is too flat and too low-liquidity to justify a fresh directional trade.
0G has squeeze fuel from negative funding, but liquidity is too poor and the active veto blocks forcing a fresh long.
GRIFFAIN is already deep into a flush and too illiquid to trust for a serious fresh short chase.
ZEN is already weak and too illiquid to trust for a serious fresh short entry after the move.
